title: linum hirsutum subsp. oreocaricum
intro: Linum hirsutum subsp. oreocaricum, also known as hairy flax, is a subspecies of the flax plant native to New Zealand. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of hairy flax include reducing inflammation and improving skin health, as well as providing antioxidant properties. Hairy flax can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile natural remedy for various health issues.
